sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.78 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: For an irreducible complex character$$of a finite group$G$, the of$$is defined by${ cod}( )=|G: |/ (1)$. Let${ Cod}(G)$be the set of irreducible character codegrees of$G$. : If$G$has an element of order$m$, then$m$divides some member of${ Cod}(G)$. The conjecture is proved when$m$is a prime power (G.\,Qian, , 97 (2011), 99--103); when$m$is square-free (I.\,M.\,Isaacs, , 97 (2011), 499--501); or wh...
